stimulus.content:
Read the passage below:

The United States Constitution is the highest law in the country. All other laws must agree with it. If a law goes against the Constitution, the courts can strike it down.

stem: What does the passage tell us about the role of the Constitution?

choices.text: It serves as the supreme law of the land
It can be changed only by the President
It applies only to the federal government
It was replaced by the Bill of Rights

answer_explanation: The passage explains that the Constitution is the highest law and all other laws must follow it. It is not changed by the President alone, it applies to all levels of government, and it was not replaced by the Bill of Rights.
